title: Why a recent supply-chain attack singled out security firms Checkmarx and Bitwarden
description: Security firms find themselves especially exposed.

title: When Robots Have Their ChatGPT Moment, Remember These Pincers
description: From sorting chicken nuggets to screwing in light bulbs, Eka’s robots are eerily lifelike. But do they have real physical smarts?

title: Coby Adcock’s Scout AI raises $100 million to train its models for war. We visited its bootcamp.
description: We visited Scout AI's training ground where it's working on AI agents that give individual soldiers control of  fleets of autonomous vehicles.

title: How AI Could Help Combat Antibiotic Resistance
description: At WIRED Health, British surgeon Ara Darzi said AI is set to transform the diagnosis and treatment of drug-resistant infections. But a lack of incentives means innovation may not reach patients.

title: It’s time to make a plan for nuclear waste
description: Today, nuclear energy enjoys a rare moment of support across the political spectrum in the US. Interest from tech companies that are scrambling to meet demand for massive data centers has sparked a resurgence of money and attention in the industry. title: At his OpenAI trial, Musk relitigates an old friendship
description: It's a story Musk has told before -- in interviews and to author Walter Isaacson for his bestselling biography of Musk -- but Tuesday was the first time he said it under oath.

title: OpenAI Really Wants Codex to Shut Up About Goblins
description: “Never talk about goblins, gremlins, raccoons, trolls, ogres, pigeons, or other animals or creatures unless it is absolutely and unambiguously relevant,” reads OpenAI’s coding agent instructions.

title: Anti-Trump Instagram pic of seashells now enough to indict ex-FBI directors
description: The clown car is all gassed up.

title: Elon Musk Testifies That He Started OpenAI to Prevent a ‘Terminator Outcome’
description: The judge also warned Musk and Sam Altman to curb their “propensity to use social media to make things worse outside the courtroom” after both sides traded attacks online.

title: Flesh-eating bacteria devour man's arm and leg in just three days
description: When doctors saw him, his limbs were discolored and crackling.

title: FCC orders review of ABC licenses after Kimmel joke offends Trump and first lady
description: Kimmel joke calling Melania an "expectant widow" followed quickly by FCC order.

title: Amazon is already offering new OpenAI products on AWS
description: A day after OpenAI got Microsoft to agree to end exclusive rights, AWS announced a slate of OpenAI model offerings, including a new agent service.

title: Drone pilot makes US rescind no-fly zones around unmarked, moving ICE vehicles
description: Civil liberty concerns spur FAA to revise drone no-fly zones near ICE vehicles.

title: Amazon launches an AI-powered audio Q&A experience on product pages
description: Amazon's new "Join the chat" feature lets you ask questions about products and receive AI-powered audio responses.

title: ‘It’s Undignified’: Hundreds of Workers Training Meta’s AI Could Be Laid Off
description: More than 700 people working for a Meta contractor in Ireland are at risk of losing their jobs, documents show.

title: Google expands Pentagon’s access to its AI after Anthropic’s refusal
description: After Anthropic refused to allow the DoD to use its AI for domestic mass surveillance and autonomous weapons, Google has signed a new contract with the department.

title: The Download: Musk and Altman’s legal showdown, and AI’s profit problem
description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. title: Elon Musk and Sam Altman are going to court over OpenAI’s future
description: After a yearslong legal feud, Elon Musk and OpenAI CEO Sam Altman are heading to trial this week in Northern California in a case that could have sweeping consequences. title: The missing step between hype and profit
description: This story originally appeared in The Algorithm, our weekly newsletter on AI. To get stories like this in your inbox first, sign up here. title: Rebuilding the data stack for AI
description: Artificial intelligence may be dominating boardroom agendas, but many enterprises are discovering that the biggest obstacle to meaningful adoption is the state of their data. title: Understanding Convolutions on Graphs
description: Understanding the building blocks and design choices of graph neural networks.

title: A Gentle Introduction to Graph Neural Networks
description: What components are needed for building learning algorithms that leverage the structure and properties of graphs?

title: Distill Hiatus
description: After five years, Distill will be taking a break.

title: Adversarial Reprogramming of Neural Cellular Automata
description: Reprogramming Neural CA to exhibit novel behaviour, using adversarial attacks.

title: Weight Banding
description: Weights in the final layer of common visual models appear as horizontal bands. We investigate how and why.
